events.label: reasoning
events.text: We need answer JSON only. Need assess scenario: commercial HVAC-R brazing & evacuation. Observed: vacuum not held at target microns (decay test - read gauge, FD). Work rough-complete. Need decide pass/fail/needs_more_info. Since vacuum decay test failed (not held at target microns), evacuation non-compliant. Decision fail. Risk? high maybe critical? Non-compliance can lead to moisture/acid, system failure, but not immediate life safety. In commercial project, high risk. Component conditions: installed-defective, non-compliant. Percent complete rough-complete maybe 80? Value numeric reading? Not provided specific microns, but task says use value for numeric reading or computed quantity when asked. Scenario says read gauge, FD but no number. Could set value to 0 or maybe null? Schema says number. We can set 0 because unknown. Or maybe value should be the target microns? Hmm. Since not provided, value 0. Confidence high 0.9. Event_time 0, rate 0, order empty, workable empty. Findings: vacuum decay test failed; system cannot be charged until leak-free evacuation verified. Actions: locate and repair leaks, re-evacuate, perform decay test to manufacturer/ACCA target, document micron reading, third-party inspection. Rationale: evacuation must achieve and hold specified microns per manufacturer/ACCA; failure indicates leaks/moisture/non-compliance. References: ACCA, EPA 608, mfr specs. Need ensure JSON valid.

  "findings": ["Vacuum decay test failed: system did not hold target micron level", "Evacuation is incomplete/non-compliant, indicating likely leaks or inadequate dehydration", "System must not be charged or closed out in this condition"],
  "actions": ["Identify and repair leaks or open joints causing vacuum loss", "Re-evacuate the system to manufacturer-specified target microns", "Repeat decay test and verify vacuum holds at target for required duration", "Document final micron readings and obtain third-party inspection before charging"],
  "rationale": "A proper evacuation must achieve and hold the manufacturer/ACCA target micron level during a decay test. A rising micron reading indicates the system is not leak-free or adequately dehydrated, which violates installation and manufacturer requirements and can cause moisture/acid damage and system failure.",
  "references": ["Manufacturer specifications", "ACCA evacuation procedures", "EPA 608 safe evacuation requirements"]
}
